name: Beware the Lizard Marsh!
author: Emythene Faldree
description:
Emythene Faldree warns travellers against the Lizard Marsh east of Daggerford — saurial ruins, black dragons, and the particular stupidity of camping on mud that breathes.

The pamphlet's woodcut illustrations are crude but accurate; several editions add a prayer to Talos that reads more like a curse.

name: Balduran Founds a City
author: Unknown
description:
Legend and municipal pride intertwine in this account of Balduran's voyages and the founding of Baldur's Gate — sea devils, buried treasure, and the first walls raised against the Sword Coast's chaos.

Historians note the text mixes myth with charter language; citizens note it reads well at Founders' Day feasts.

name: Bestiary of Creatures Strange and Wonderful
author: Anonymous
description:
Bestiary of Creatures Strange and Wonderful — Natural history from hunters, riverfolk, and sages who went outside and wrote down what bit them. Anonymous is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 600s DR.

Seasonal addenda in surviving copies suggest the author revised the work every year they lived to see spring.

name: Brewing Ale, Beer, and Wine
author: Irym Dyleth
description:
Brewing Ale, Beer, and Wine — Commercial wisdom dressed as prose — prices, routes, guild customs, and when to walk away from a deal. Irym Dyleth is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1370 DR.

Factors along the Long Road keep folded excerpts in strongboxes next to their scales and seals.

name: Book of Vile Darkness
author: Vecna
description:
Book of Vile Darkness — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Vecna is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to unknown.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Bowgentle's Book
author: Bowgentle
description:
Bowgentle's Book — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Bowgentle is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to unknown.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Breyer's Draconic Investigations
author: Breyer
description:
Breyer's Draconic Investigations — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Breyer is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1480 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.

name: Ballads and Lore of One Dusty Road
author: Sharanralee Crownstar
description:
Ballads and Lore of One Dusty Road — An in-universe work listed in Candlekeep indices and frontier libraries throughout the Heartlands. Sharanralee Crownstar is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1337 DR.

Copies vary by scriptorium; Candlekeep treats the title as known though completeness is never guaranteed.
